outcome_text: The AI model is explained, validated, and documented, and AI system output is interpreted within its context—as identified in the MAP function—to inform responsible use and governance.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI model explanation, validation, and contextual interpretation, including developing model explanations appropriate for different audiences, validating model behavior and outputs, and interpreting AI system outputs within their operational context to support responsible use and governance decisions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI model explanation, validation, and contextual interpretation, including developing model explanations appropriate for different audiences, validating model behavior and outputs, and interpreting AI system outputs within their operational context to support responsible use and governance decisions.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.9 (MEASURE) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-004 is the strongest match (STS: 0.703, cosine: 0.697). Secondary: AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.693), AG-K-006 (AG, cosine: 0.671).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.703). Shared vocabulary: [ai, knowledge, responsible]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [appropriate, audiences, behavior, context, contextual, decisions] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, explainability, fairness, human, impact, methodologies] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.879 (P=0.872, R=0.887). Balanced P/R — the 38-word competency implication and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.697 ≈ STS 0.703 (gap 0.007). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, knowledge, responsible] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge, responsible]. NIST AI RMF-only: [appropriate, audiences, behavior, context, contextual, decisions]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, explainability, fairness, human, impact, methodologies].
